meta.title: cede

source.publication: Section 4: Roots, Prefixes, Suffixes and Foreign Words (owner-supplied reference)
source.usage_note: Personal study only. Root, words and meanings transcribed faithfully from the source PDF. Parts, glosses, context sentences and construct options are the teaching layer, authored for the Language Garden (LANGUAGE_GARDEN_BIBLE §10).

meta.reviewer_notes: The garden's opening plant: the Bible's own worked example (§5.1, §10). secede is the anchor; intercede is the Bible's own Reach word.

root.label: cede
root.mentor_note: cede shows up wherever something moves, yields, or gives way, often quietly.

context_sentences: Several provinces threatened to secede after the ruling divided the coalition beyond repair.
By the following spring, two more member states had voted to secede from the union entirely.
A long and quiet negotiation would precede any public announcement of the merger.
In the manuscript, a short preface always seemed to precede the author's more difficult chapters.
As the drought continued, the shoreline began to recede further from the old fishing docks.
Her doubts started to recede once the committee finally published its full report.
The junior diplomat chose to intercede quietly before the dispute reached the ambassador's desk.
No one expected the retired judge to intercede on behalf of a stranger.

construct_options.text: To step in between two sides, on someone's behalf
To go far ahead of everyone else
To refuse to go along with a decision

context_sentences: After an hour of resistance, the committee finally chose to concede the point.
It took real courage for the senator to concede that the earlier forecast had been wrong.

construct_options.text: To fully give way and admit something is true
To go somewhere together with a large group
To leave a place for good
